item.title: The leverage layer cake behind HSBC’s private credit losses
item.description: HSBC did not lend to collapsed mortgage provider MFS. It took a $400mn hit anyway

item.title: Musk tried to recruit Altman for role at Tesla before falling out at OpenAI
item.description: Testimony from billionaire’s confidante Shivon Zilis reveals wrangling over the future of the AI lab that led to lawsuit

item.title: FirstFT: North Korea abandons goal of reuniting with South
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: DeepSeek nears $45bn valuation, and China calls for Iran war ceasefire

item.title: Airbus wins $19bn order in Canada’s biggest ever aircraft deal
item.description: AirAsia orders 150 A220-300 airliners in big boost for Canadian manufacturing

item.title: US prosecutors charge 30 with insider trading on law firm deal materials
item.description: Justice department says lawyers from elite Wall Street firms aided scheme with confidential transaction details

item.title: Duke of Westminster’s real estate group to sell properties after losses in US
item.description: Grosvenor takes writedowns on North American sites and pledges to reinvest sale proceeds in joint ventures

item.title: Carlyle teams with Diversified Energy on $1.2bn oil and gas venture
item.description: Private equity firm seeks to securitise future revenue from well production to private credit investors

item.title: FanDuel owner profits as a market-maker for prediction platforms
item.description: CEO Peter Jackson says Flutter expects new business to become ‘good contributor to our revenues’

item.title: Arm projects $2bn in sales of its new AI chip from next year
item.description: SoftBank-backed UK group says its first in-house semiconductor has drawn strong demand

item.title: Ted Turner, media mogul, 1938-2026
item.description: The founder of CNN changed the news industry — and lived large along the way

item.title: Tabloid tale of ‘sex slave’ banker sets Wall Street imaginations aflutter
item.description: Roles reversed in claim by ex-JPMorgan employee in industry where misconduct accusations almost always involve men

item.title: EU weighs get-out for gas leaks after US and fossil fuel industry pressure
item.description: Guidelines would see penalties suspended during energy crises

item.title: Rival airports lose legal challenge over private jets at RAF Northolt
item.description: High Court judge dismisses claim of ‘unfair disparity’ in safety standards

item.title: US fuel exports hit record in boon for oil companies and threat to Trump
item.description: Hormuz closure prompts surge in buying from Asia and Europe

item.title: Intertek primed to reject EQT’s latest £10bn takeover bid
item.description: FTSE 100 testing company says Swedish private equity group’s fresh approach still undervalues the business

item.title: America owes its European allies a defence roadmap
item.description: Retaliatory cutbacks are no way to achieve proper burden-shifting in Nato

item.title: SpaceX reaches deal to rent data centre space to Anthropic
item.description: AI start-up is racing to add computing power to keep up with its growth

item.title: Poland probes record number of Russian espionage cases
item.description: Warsaw has carried out as many counter-intelligence investigations over past two years as in the previous 30

item.title: Spain to repatriate 150 passengers from cruise hit by rat-borne virus
item.description: Hantavirus outbreak has killed three passengers on ship, which is en route to Tenerife

item.title: Novo Nordisk gets a second chance at weight-loss supremacy
item.description: The Danish drugmaker appears to have the edge in GLP-1 pills

item.title: Call my agents: snapshots from the future of work
item.description: Staff are often more willing than leaders to move quickly on AI and automation

item.title: CNN founder Turner dies at 87
item.description: Flamboyant billionaire created first 24-hour news network in US that transformed media and political landscape

item.title: Morgan Stanley to launch spot crypto trading on E*Trade platform
item.description: Wall Street bank becomes one of a handful of big lenders to offer service

item.title: Landlords and second-home buyers squeezed by stamp duty surcharge
item.description: Buyers of additional properties now pay the lion’s share of SDLT in most English local authorities

item.title: Disney revenues grow as higher spending offsets parks slowdown
item.description: Increases in cruises and streaming divisions help group mitigate impact of falling visitors to US theme parks

item.title: Uber hit by Middle East conflict despite strong bookings
item.description: Ride-hailing company posts weaker than expected first-quarter revenues but issues optimistic outlook

item.title: PayPal, Mastercard and Visa probed over alleged UK antitrust breaches
item.description: Move is rare exercise of Financial Conduct Authority’s competition law powers

item.title: CNN’s Amanpour airs concerns for news channel’s independence under Ellison
item.description: Presenter cites ‘ideological realignment’ of rival CBS after it was acquired by billionaire with close ties to Trump

item.title: AI ‘losers’ should be compensated through retraining, says ex-cabinet secretary
item.description: Gus O’Donnell calls for funding to teach new skills to people who lose their jobs as a result of the new technology

item.title: Ex-WHSmith high street business to shut up to 150 stores
item.description: Owner Modella Capital launches deep restructuring of TG Jones less than one year after buying chain
